tasks.subject: [external-mcp-access] [P1] Add mcp section to UserConfigSchema and SENSITIVE_CONFIG_KEYS
tasks.description:
Add the `mcp` config section to `UserConfigSchema` in `packages/shared/src/config-schema.ts` and register `'mcp.apiKey'` as a sensitive key.

## Implementation

### 1. Add `'mcp.apiKey'` to SENSITIVE_CONFIG_KEYS (line 8, after `'tunnel.passcodeSalt'`):

```typescript
export const SENSITIVE_CONFIG_KEYS = [
  'tunnel.authtoken',
  'tunnel.auth',
  'tunnel.passcodeHash',
  'tunnel.passcodeSalt',
  'mcp.apiKey',
] as const;
```

### 2. Add `mcp` section to `UserConfigSchema` (between the `extensions` field and the `sessionSecret` field):

```typescript
mcp: z
  .object({
    enabled: z.boolean().default(true),
    apiKey: z.string().nullable().default(null),
    rateLimit: z
      .object({
        enabled: z.boolean().default(true),
        maxPerWindow: z.number().int().min(1).max(1000).default(60),
        windowSecs: z.number().int().min(1).max(3600).default(60),
      })
      .default(() => ({ enabled: true, maxPerWindow: 60, windowSecs: 60 })),
  })
  .default(() => ({
    enabled: true,
    apiKey: null,
    rateLimit: { enabled: true, maxPerWindow: 60, windowSecs: 60 },
  })),
```

This follows the exact same pattern as the `tunnel` section (boolean flags + nullable secret + nested config object with `.default(...)`).

No manual update to `USER_CONFIG_DEFAULTS` is needed — it is derived from `UserConfigSchema.parse({ version: 1 })`, so the `.default(...)` values are picked up automatically.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `SENSITIVE_CONFIG_KEYS` includes `'mcp.apiKey'` — PATCH requests that modify `mcp.apiKey` will include a warning in the response
- `UserConfigSchema.parse({ version: 1 })` produces a config with `mcp.enabled === true`, `mcp.apiKey === null`, and `mcp.rateLimit === { enabled: true, maxPerWindow: 60, windowSecs: 60 }`
- `USER_CONFIG_DEFAULTS.mcp` is correctly populated with default values
- Existing PATCH tests continue to pass (the new `mcp` section has defaults, so no existing tests break)
- `pnpm typecheck` passes across all packages
tasks.activeForm: Adding mcp section to UserConfigSchema and SENSITIVE_CONFIG_KEYS

tasks.subject: [external-mcp-access] [P3] Add ExternalMcpCard to ToolsTab with toggle, auth, rate limiting, setup instructions, and duplicate warning
tasks.description:
Add the External Access card to `apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/ui/ToolsTab.tsx` as a new `CollapsibleFieldCard` below the existing tool groups `FieldCard`. This is the full frontend UI for external MCP access controls. Note: `ToolsTab.tsx` is currently 433 lines. The spec calls for an extracted `ExternalMcpCard.tsx` component to keep files under the 500-line threshold. Create `apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/ui/ExternalMcpCard.tsx` for the card content, imported and rendered in `ToolsTab.tsx`. ## Implementation ### Component Structure ``` ToolsTab (existing) ... existing FieldCard ... <- NEW ExternalMcpCard trigger: "External Access" + status badge + switch content: - read-only URL with copy button - API key lifecycle - toggle + config inputs - tabbed per-client snippets - amber alert callout ``` ### ExternalMcpCard Component (`ExternalMcpCard.tsx`) ```tsx import { useState, useCallback } from 'react'; import { useQueryClient } from '@tanstack/react-query'; import { AlertTriangle, ChevronDown, Copy, Check } from 'lucide-react'; import { Badge, Button, Collapsible, CollapsibleContent, CollapsibleTrigger, FieldCard, FieldCardContent, Input, SettingRow, Switch, Tabs, TabsContent, TabsList, TabsTrigger, } from '@/layers/shared/ui'; import { cn } from '@/layers/shared/lib'; import { useTransport } from '@/layers/shared/model'; ``` Props: receives `serverConfig` (the MCP section from server config) and `transport`. ### Collapsed Trigger Row The collapsed row shows: - Label: "External Access" - Description: "Allow external agents to use DorkOS tools via MCP" - Right side: Status badge + Switch + Chevron Badge logic: - `Enabled` (green outline: `border-green-500/50 text-green-600`) when `enabled && authConfigured` - `No auth` (amber: `border-amber-500/50 text-amber-600`) when `enabled && !authConfigured` - `Disabled` (secondary variant) when `!enabled` The switch toggles `mcp.enabled` via `transport.updateConfig({ mcp: { ...currentMcpConfig, enabled: newValue } })` and invalidates the `['config']` query. ### Endpoint Row ```tsx
{endpoint}
``` The `endpoint` value comes from `serverConfig.mcp.endpoint` (e.g., `http://localhost:6242/mcp`). Use the existing `useCopy()` hook pattern from ServerTab for the copy button, or implement a small inline CopyButton: ```tsx function CopyButton({ value }: { value: string }) { const [copied, setCopied] = useState(false); const copy = useCallback(() => { navigator.clipboard.writeText(value); setCopied(true); setTimeout(() => setCopied(false), 2000); }, [value]); return ( ); } ``` ### Auth Section Three states based on `authSource` and `authConfigured`: **State 1: No API key configured (`authSource === 'none'`)** ```tsx ``` **State 2: API key from config (`authSource === 'config'`)** ```tsx
{newlyGeneratedKey ? ( <> {newlyGeneratedKey} ) : ( dork_{'\u2022'.repeat(8)} )}
{newlyGeneratedKey && (

Copy this key now — it won't be shown again.

)} ``` **State 3: API key from env var (`authSource === 'env'`)** ```tsx Environment variable ``` `generateKey` calls `transport.generateMcpApiKey()` and stores the returned key in local state (`newlyGeneratedKey`). On subsequent config re-fetches, the key is no longer available (only `authConfigured: true` is returned). `rotateKey` calls `transport.deleteMcpApiKey()` then `transport.generateMcpApiKey()` in sequence. ### Rate Limit Section ```tsx {rateLimit.enabled && ( <> updateRateLimit({ maxPerWindow: parseInt(e.target.value, 10) })} className="w-20" /> updateRateLimit({ windowSecs: parseInt(e.target.value, 10) })} className="w-20" /> )} ``` Rate limit changes go through `transport.updateConfig({ mcp: { ...currentMcp, rateLimit: { ...currentRateLimit, ...patch } } })` and invalidate `['config']`. ### Setup Instructions (Tabbed) A `Tabs` component showing per-client config snippets. Each tab has a copy button for the entire snippet. Tab: Claude Code ```json { "mcpServers": { "dorkos-external": { "type": "http", "url": "{endpoint}", "headers": { "Authorization": "Bearer {apiKeyOrPlaceholder}" } } } } ``` Plus CLI command: `claude mcp add-json dorkos-external '{"type":"http","url":"{endpoint}","headers":{"Authorization":"Bearer {apiKeyOrPlaceholder}"}}'` Tab: Cursor ```json { "mcpServers": { "dorkos-external": { "url": "{endpoint}", "headers": { "Authorization": "Bearer {apiKeyOrPlaceholder}" } } } } ``` Tab: Windsurf ```json { "mcpServers": { "dorkos-external": { "serverUrl": "{endpoint}", "headers": { "Authorization": "Bearer {apiKeyOrPlaceholder}" } } } } ``` When an API key exists (either `newlyGeneratedKey` from local state or `authConfigured: true`), use the actual key in snippets if available, otherwise show `dork_YOUR_API_KEY` as placeholder. The server name `dorkos-external` is deliberate to avoid collision with the internal `dorkos` MCP server name. ### Duplicate Tool Warning Amber callout at the bottom: ```tsx

Do not configure this for agents running inside DorkOS.

DorkOS already provides tools to its managed agents internally. Adding DorkOS as an external MCP server to those same agents causes duplicate tool names — the Anthropic API will return HTTP 400 "Tool names must be unique" and all tool calls will fail. External MCP access is for agents running outside of DorkOS (standalone Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf).

``` ### Integration in ToolsTab.tsx Import and render after the existing `FieldCard`: ```tsx import { ExternalMcpCard } from './ExternalMcpCard'; // In the return JSX, after the closing : ``` The `ExternalMcpCard` reads `serverConfig` via its own `useQuery` or receives it as a prop from `ToolsTab`. ## Acceptance Criteria - External Access card renders below the tool groups card in ToolsTab - Collapsed state shows label, description, status badge, and toggle switch - Toggle switch enables/disables MCP access via `updateConfig` - Endpoint row shows the MCP URL with a copy button - Auth section shows "Generate API Key" when no key exists - Auth section shows masked key + Rotate button when config key exists - Auth section shows "Environment variable" badge when key source is env - Rate limit section has enable toggle and conditional max/window inputs - Setup instructions show tabbed snippets for Claude Code, Cursor, and Windsurf - Snippets auto-fill the endpoint URL and API key when available - Duplicate tool warning is displayed as an amber callout - All config changes invalidate the `['config']` query for fresh data - File stays under 500 lines (ExternalMcpCard is in its own file) - `pnpm typecheck` and `pnpm lint` pass
tasks.activeForm: Adding ExternalMcpCard to ToolsTab with full external access controls
